summary: Decompiled and exposed more of the Game & Watch Judge item article, adding spawn/orientation/animation-removal logic and reordering functions to match binary layout. The decomp-dev bot reported five unmatched-item improvements, including itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim to 95.73%, it_802C78B8 to 97.82%, and it_802C7774 to 90.86%. No human review comments were present in the slice.

key_files.role: Added implementations for Judge item spawn, facing/orientation update, animation removal check, and reordered callbacks.
Added address-tagged prototypes for it_802C7774, it_802C78B8, and itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim.

what_changed: Added it_802C7774, a Game & Watch Judge item spawn helper that fills a SpawnItem for It_Kind_GameWatch_Judge, sets parent pointers, facing direction, zero velocity/damage, x44_flag.b0, and then calls item setup helpers including Item_80268B18, Item_8026AB54, it_80273670, it_802C78B8, db_80225DD8, and it_8027CE64.
Added it_802C78B8, which reads the item's owner, gets owner facing through ftLib_800865C0, obtains a JObj grandchild, and sets Y rotation to M_PI when facing is 1.0f or 0.0f otherwise.

Added itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim, which refreshes the Judge item's facing-dependent rotation, checks ftGw_SpecialS_ItemCheckJudgementRemove when an owner exists, treats missing owner as removable, sets the Game & Watch judgement flag before removal, and returns whether the item should be removed.
Moved itGameWatchJudge_Logic77_Destroyed earlier and itGameWatchJudge_Logic77_EvtUnk later in the source, matching the stated goal of reordering functions to match binary layout.
Added required includes for math, db, ftlib, it/forward, and it/it_2725 support used by the new functions.
Updated itgamewatchjudge.h with prototypes and address comments for the newly decompiled functions.
smart_moves: Kept functions in binary/address order, matching the PR body note that functions were reordered to match binary layout.
Used existing item and fighter helpers rather than open-coding article behavior: Item_80268B18, Item_8026AB54, it_80273670, db_80225DD8, it_8027CE64, ftLib_800865C0, and itGetJObjGrandchild.
Preserved owner-null guards in destroy, animation, and orientation paths, avoiding item cleanup behavior that would dereference a missing owner.
Used explicit PAD_STACK calls and an awkward temporary assignment in itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim, which are likely matching-oriented choices supported by the diff.
Exposed new prototypes with address comments in the header so other files can call the decompiled item article helpers while retaining address-based traceability.
decomp_lessons: For item article spawn helpers, fully initialize SpawnItem fields in the same order as the target code: prev_pos/pos, kind, facing_dir, damage, velocity, parent gobj slots, flags, and x40.
Facing-based article model orientation can be matched through owner facing from ftLib_800865C0 and a grandchild JObj rotation using HSD_JObjSetRotationY with M_PI or 0.0f.

Do not assume duplicated-looking logic should be factored out during matching work; it_802C78B8 and itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim both contain similar facing-rotation code, likely because the binary does.
Function definition order can matter for decomp workflow and binary layout comparison; this PR explicitly reordered callbacks to match layout.
Awkward locals, inner scopes, casts, and PAD_STACK usage are valid matching tactics when trying to recover function percentages before cleanup or renaming.
naming_conventions: Unknown or not-yet-semantic item helpers stayed address-based: it_802C7774 and it_802C78B8.
Callbacks use item logic naming such as itGameWatchJudge_Logic77_Destroyed and itGameWatchJudge_Logic77_EvtUnk.
The new animation callback is named itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim in the diff; note its casing differs from itGameWatchJudge.
Header parameter names are more semantic than the C definition in one case: owner_gobj, bone_index, and judge_num correspond to parent_gobj, part, and arg4 in the implementation.
The item kind constant used for spawning is It_Kind_GameWatch_Judge.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(4) in it_802C7774 and PAD_STACK(8) in itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim.
Reordered functions by address: new prototypes show 2C7774, 2C78B8, 2C7A54, 2C7A84, 2C7AD0, 2C7AF0, 2C7B10, 2C7B54, and 2C7D40 layout.
Used the matching-oriented expression owner = (new_var = GET_ITEM(item_gobj))->owner even though new_var is otherwise not semantically meaningful in the excerpt.
Used explicit casts such as (Fighter_GObj*) parent_gobj and (Item_GObj*) jobj to satisfy call signatures and likely match codegen.
Converted judge argument with (float) (arg4 + 1) before passing it to it_80273670.
Retrieved special attributes as void** and passed *special_attrs to it_8027CE64, preserving the observed pointer-indirection pattern.
review_feedback: Author note/body: "reordered some funcs to match binary layout too".

decomp-dev bot reported five GALE01 improvements for main/melee/it/items/itgamewatchjudge: itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim 0.00% to 95.73%, it_802C78B8 0.00% to 97.82%, it_802C7774 0.00% to 90.86%, plus .sdata2 and .sdata improvements.
No human review comments or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for renames or semantic cleanup of it_802C7774, it_802C78B8, and itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim.
Check Game & Watch SpecialS callsites to confirm the semantics of bone_index/part and judge_num/arg4 in it_802C7774.
Look for subsequent matching passes that raise it_802C7774, it_802C78B8, or itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim from the reported 90-98% range to full match.
Compare other item article spawn helpers for reusable SpawnItem initialization and post-spawn setup order.
